items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Weigh on Global Markets
items.summary: U.S. stock futures trended lower early Thursday as rising oil prices, fueled by escalating conflict in the Middle East, impacted investor sentiment. Brent crude surged towards $100 a barrel, intensifying concerns over inflation and global supply chains. While overall market sentiment remains cautious, a slight optimism persists amidst mixed economic signals.

items.statContext: Down 0.41% on March 12, 2026. Futures were down 0.50% pre-market.
Gained 0.08% on Wednesday, March 11. Futures were down 0.49% pre-market on March 12.
Fell 0.61% on Wednesday, March 11. Futures were down 0.59% pre-market on March 12.

items.headline: Bumble Surges on Strong Q4 Performance
items.summary: Shares of the dating app company rallied after exceeding top-line expectations in its fourth-quarter earnings report.

items.headline: Oracle Jumps on Robust Earnings
items.summary: The tech giant saw its stock climb after reporting stronger-than-expected profit and revenue for its latest quarter, partly driven by demand for cloud computing in AI.

items.headline: Sprinklr Beats Earnings Estimates
items.summary: Sprinklr Inc. shares gained after the company posted fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $0.13 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.10 per share.

items.headline: Cadre Holdings Plunges on Q4 Miss
items.summary: Cadre Holdings Inc. shares fell significantly after reporting fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $0.27 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.41 per share.

items.headline: Campbell Soup Cuts Forecasts, Shares Decline
items.summary: Campbell Soup Company's stock dropped after the company reported a weaker profit for the latest quarter than analysts expected and cut its annual forecasts, citing struggles in its snack business and tariff pressures.

items.headline: AeroVironment Down on Disappointing Profit Outlook
items.summary: The defense contractor's shares declined after forecasting 2026 adjusted profit below market estimates.

items.headline: PPI Data Released, Jobless Claims Due
items.summary: The Producer Price Index (PPI) data for February 2026, including core figures, was released this morning. Later today, the U.S. will release its weekly unemployment claims figures, a key indicator for labor market health.

items.headline: U.S. Trade Deficit Narrows in January
items.summary: The U.S. Census Bureau and Bureau of Economic Analysis announced that the goods and services deficit in January 2026 was $54.5 billion, a notable decrease of 25.3% from December.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Ahead of March Meeting
items.summary: Investors widely anticipate the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged at its upcoming policy meeting on March 17-18. Policymakers are assessing the risks of rising energy prices alongside signs of a cooling labor market.

items.headline: KLA Corporation Announces Share Buyback and Dividend Increase
items.summary: KLA Corporation ($KLAC) held its Investor Day, announcing a new $7 billion share repurchase program and a 21% increase in its quarterly dividend to $2.30 per share. The company also reaffirmed its March 2026 guidance.

items.headline: BMW Group Reports Stable 2025 Earnings Amid Headwinds
items.summary: The BMW Group announced stable Group earnings before tax of over €10 billion in 2025 despite significant headwinds, including tariffs. However, the company anticipates a moderate decline in Group earnings before tax for 2026 due to higher tariffs and raw material costs.

items.headline: Oil and Gas, Technology Lead US Sectors Yesterday
items.summary: On Wednesday, Energy, Technology, and Communication Services were the only U.S. sectors to close in positive territory, with clean energy funds also performing strongly. Broader market weakness was observed across other sectors.
